name: Space Structures — Military & Industrial
description: 10 structures — The backbone of interstellar power: shipyards that birth fleets, refineries that process the fuel of empires, prison stations that hold the worst offenders, and military fortresses that guard the gates between stars.

vehicles.name: Orbital Shipyard
vehicles.desc: Massive construction facility where warships are built from keel to commissioning in zero-gravity drydocks. Skeletal gantries cradle half-finished hulls, and the constant blue flicker of welding arcs makes her look like a constellation of earthbound stars. She can build anything from frigates to capital ships given time and materials, and her destruction would cripple a navy's ability to replace losses for years. The workforce numbers in the tens of thousands — engineers, welders, systems specialists — and they take a craftsman's pride in the vessels they launch.
vehicles.notes: Ship construction. Multiple drydock bays. Critical strategic asset. Lightly armed for size.

vehicles.name: Drydock Platform
vehicles.desc: Repair and refit facility designed to service capital ships between deployments. She clamps onto a vessel's hull with magnetic grapples and extends pressurised work tunnels that give repair crews access to external systems without EVA. A damaged cruiser can be stripped, repaired, and returned to service in weeks rather than the months a shipyard would require. She's the fleet's field hospital, and during wartime she never sleeps.
vehicles.notes: Ship repair. Can service vessels up to Size 22. Faster than shipyard but less capable.

vehicles.name: Fuel Depot
vehicles.desc: Refuelling station positioned at strategic waypoints along major trade routes. Her storage tanks hold thousands of tonnes of refined fuel, and her pumping systems can replenish a capital ship's reserves in hours. She's automated to the point where a crew of twenty can operate the entire facility, though the insurance company insists on a minimum of fifty. Explosively vulnerable — a single torpedo hit on the main tankage would create a fireball visible from orbit.
vehicles.notes: Strategic fuel reserve. Extremely vulnerable to attack. Small crew. Explosive.

vehicles.name: Mining Platform
vehicles.desc: Industrial extraction facility anchored to an asteroid or positioned in a planetary ring system. Enormous drills bore into rock while processing equipment separates valuable ores from waste material. The refined output is packed into cargo pods and launched towards collection points by electromagnetic catapult. Her crew works three-month rotations in conditions that make offshore oil rigs look luxurious, and the accident rate is something the company prefers not to publicise.
vehicles.notes: Resource extraction. Hazardous working conditions. Anchored to asteroid. Industrial.

vehicles.name: Orbital Refinery
vehicles.desc: Processing facility that converts raw ore and gas into refined materials for shipbuilding, fuel production, and industrial manufacturing. She sits at the centre of a web of cargo routes, receiving raw materials from mining platforms and shipping finished products to shipyards and colonies. The refining process generates enormous heat, and her radiator arrays extend for kilometres, glowing cherry-red against the black. She smells of hot metal and chemistry even through the environmental systems.
vehicles.notes: EVE archetype. Industrial processing. Critical supply chain node. Moderate defences.

vehicles.name: Military Fortress
vehicles.desc: Purpose-built defensive installation bristling with weapons batteries and protected by multiple layers of armour and shielding. She guards chokepoints — jump gates, system entries, orbital approaches to strategic worlds — and she's designed to hold against sustained fleet assault until reinforcements arrive. Her crew lives in spartan conditions behind metres of composite armour, and her magazines hold enough ammunition to fight for weeks. Besieging her is expensive. Bypassing her is impossible.
vehicles.notes: Dedicated defence. Heavy weapons. Deep magazines. Designed to withstand fleet assault.

vehicles.name: Prison Station
vehicles.desc: Orbital penal facility positioned deliberately far from inhabited worlds, where the most dangerous criminals serve sentences measured in decades. She's built like a fortress turned inside out — the weapons point inward as much as outward, and the security systems are designed to prevent escape rather than repel attack. Guards rotate every three months because longer postings produce sympathy, corruption, or madness. The inmates have a saying: the stars are close enough to see and too far to reach.
vehicles.notes: Maximum security. Remote location. Inward-facing defences. 500-2,000 inmates.

vehicles.name: Weapons Testing Range
vehicles.desc: Remote facility where new weapon systems are developed, tested, and certified for fleet use. She's surrounded by a debris field of destroyed target drones and failed prototypes, and her sensor arrays record every detonation in exquisite detail. Access is restricted, her location is classified, and the scientists who work here sign non-disclosure agreements that outlive them. The explosions are spectacular. Nobody outside the programme ever sees them.
vehicles.notes: R&D facility. Classified location. Surrounded by debris field. Moderate defences.

vehicles.name: Jump Gate
vehicles.desc: Artificial wormhole generator that creates stable transit points between star systems. She's the most expensive single piece of infrastructure in known space, and the political control of jump gates is the foundation of interstellar power. Her ring structure accelerates exotic matter to relativistic velocities, tearing a hole in spacetime that ships can traverse in seconds. Construction takes decades. Destruction takes moments. Neither is undertaken lightly.
vehicles.notes: FTL transit infrastructure. Enormous strategic value. Unarmed — defended by fleet presence.

vehicles.name: Gas Giant Skimmer
vehicles.desc: Industrial platform that skims the upper atmosphere of gas giant planets, harvesting hydrogen and helium-3 for fuel production. She rides the boundary between space and atmosphere, dipping into cloud layers that would crush a conventional vessel and climbing back to orbit with tanks full of raw materials. Her hull is reinforced against pressure and corrosive atmospheric chemistry, and her crew monitors the weather below with the nervous attention of sailors watching a storm front approach.
vehicles.notes: Atmospheric mining. Gas giant operations. Pressure-hardened hull. Fuel production.
